understanding.goal_understanding: The goal was to complete each level by positioning every selectable piece onto the goal tiles marked with '+', with the requirement that all goals in a level must be occupied simultaneously for the engine to report WIN; finishing all levels would complete the game.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: The currently selected piece (shown as 'P' in the semantic grid) could be moved one grid cell per MOVE action (coordinates are in 3-pixel increments), with walls '#' blocking movement depending on their collision behavior. Some adjacent objects (notably blocks 'B') could be pushable: attempting to move into them would push them if space allowed. Levels could contain multiple selectables; CLICK with an exact on-screen/pixel coordinate of another selectable switched control to that piece (changing player.id/position), enabling coordinated placement onto multiple goals within the step budget.
